summary: Added local registry alias normalization for Polymarket/Kalshi abbreviations CWS->CHW and OAK->ATH before ESPN registry matching.
Kept the patch scoped to recommendation_api.py only and preserved existing discovery, model, and execution behavior.
Verification showed HOU@OAK now surfaces as a candidate mapped to HOU vs ATH instead of dropping as no_registry_match.
tests_run: Ran the shadow loop once and inspected discovery logs for registry-match behavior and duplicate_event_market count.
result: Healthy candidate log appeared for event=mlb-hou-oak-2026-04-04 as HOU vs ATH.
duplicate_event_market remained 0 in the discovery summary.

TOR@CWS did not appear as a same-day live candidate in this run because the available tor-cws event was dated 2026-04-05 and matched a FINAL/non-live registry game, so no same-day healthy candidate line was available to observe during verification.
risks: Alias normalization is currently local to recommendation_api.py by task restriction; if future registry matching happens elsewhere, the same alias map may need to be applied in another allowed task.
TOR@CWS acceptance could not be fully observed live in this specific verification window because no same-day live tor-cws event was present in the returned discovery set.
